REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS A. BAILEY AND ANJULANT REAL ESTATE PROPERTIES LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED UNDER MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B). SUGASAWA V. NEWLAND (1994) 27 CAL. APP. 4TH 294 WHILE PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO BE WILLING TO EXPEND RESOURCES ON OBTAINING A DEFAULT JUDGEMENT BEFORE DEFENDANT MAY BRING THIS MOTION FOR MANDATORY RELIEF, THE COURT IS NOT INCLINED TO WASTE ITS RESOURCES ON CONDUCTING A PROVE-UP HEARING FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT, WHICH WILL THEN BE VACATED UNDER MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B). COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT ADMITTED FAULT AND MOVED FOR MANDATORY RELIEF; THERE IS NOTHING IN THE RECORD TO INDICATE THAT HE WOULD NOT DO THE SAME AFTER THE JUDGEMENT IS ENTERED. Johnson V Bailey Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Aug-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samuel Lee Pooler (510-879-7560) for Plaintiff/opposing party Charles Johnson.
Mr. Bloomfield (415-409-7611) for Defendant/Moving party A. BAILEY and ANJULANT REAL ESTATE PROPERTIES LLC.

Hearing is held. Aregued re: attorney fees and time to respond. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs A. BAILEY and ANJULANT REAL ESTATE PROPERTIES LLC's Motion To Set Aside Default is GRANTED under mandatory provisions of CCP 473(b). Sugasawa v. Newland (1994) 27 Cal. App. 4th 294 While Plaintiff appears to be willing to expend resources on obtaining a default judgement before Defendant may bring this motion for mandatory relief, the Court is not inclined to waste its resources on conducting a prove-up hearing for entry of judgment, which will then be vacated under mandatory provisions of CCP 473(b). Counsel for Defendant admitted fault and moved for mandatory relief; there is nothing in the record to indicate that he would not do the same after the judgement is entered. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
